name: Old-Time Popcorn Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. Remove all unpopped kernels from popped corn.
3. Put popcorn in a large roasting pan; keep warm in the oven.
4. Butter the sides of a 2-quart saucepan.
5. In the saucepan, combine sugar, water, light corn syrup, vinegar, and salt.
6. Cook to 270°F (soft-crack stage), stirring frequently (the mixture should boil gently over the entire surface).
7. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla.
8. Slowly pour the mixture over hot popcorn.
9. Stir just until mixed.
10. Butter hands; using a buttered cup, scoop up popcorn mixture.
11. Shape with buttered hands into 2-1/2 to 3-inch balls.
From the recipe files of Sylvia Steiger, GEnie THE.STEIGERS, CI$ 71511,2253, GT Cookbook echo moderator at net/node 004/005
